parity.notes: Counted on the reference solutions (both arms structurally parallel): 1 static class/module + 15 functions. Complexity = 1 per function + 1 per branch/loop: EntryFee 3, IsValidAccount 1, IsValidAmount 1, PostEntry 1, AccountBalance 4, TotalDebited 3, TotalCredited 3, TotalVolume 2, FeesCollected 1, TransferCost 3, CanAfford 1, Transfer 2, BatchPost 2, BatchTransfer 5, ReverseEntry 1 = 33, +1 = 34. Third-wave hardness model (scale + interaction, per epochs/hardness-check-001/VERDICT.md): modification-under-constraint on a complete working 14-function double-entry ledger. The fee schedule is computed at three sites (EntryFee helper; inlined on the costing path in TransferCost/CanAfford; inlined on the batch path in BatchTransfer) - a naive fee change updates only the helper and silently breaks batch totals, transfer-cost symmetry, and affordability boundaries, all probed by held-out tests on distant functions. Starting fixture compiles and passes the preserved-behavior subset conceptually; held-out suite is red until the modification lands (ReverseEntry absent from the starting fixture). NOT yet difficulty-validated (gates doc 3.1/3.6). timeoutSeconds is 900 (vs the 600 default) because the modification touches three inlined fee sites plus batch paths across a 14-function fixture — honest attempts routinely need more wall-clock than the small wave-1/2 pairs, and a 600s cap would censor them.

wedgeWhitelistCheck: Calor-arm contracts: all preconditions are binary comparisons over i32 parameters, signed 32-bit integer literals, arithmetic (+ count 1), (+ count 2), (+ count n), (* 2 n), and array length of simple variable bases via LEN (modeled-forms 1: i32 -> BitVec32, T[] -> Array with u32 $length companion; 2: binary operators, integer literals, simple references). Postconditions: (>= result 0), (>= result amount), (>= result (+ count 1)), (== result (+ count 1)). No function calls, no floats, no strings, no computed array bases, no generic-typed values appear in any contract. Mutating functions (PostEntry, Transfer, BatchPost, BatchTransfer, ReverseEntry) declare E{mut} (heap mutation); pure functions declare E{}. No manifest-checked BCL effect boundaries are crossed (W1 category), so no effect-manifest coverage is required beyond mut enforcement, which is compiler-checked.
